title: Level 336
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=GFS+Didot&family=Inconsolata:wdth,wght@112.5,700&display=swap'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: Inconsolata; }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 336: "Undecided Fate // Senseless Salvation"}} {{Transparent Theme}} {{Monochromatic Theme}} {{Image Banner|caption=Life sometimes is really surreal.
Regardless, it was fun while it lasted.
Sometimes, you just have to concede.
A new tomorrow or the end of your story?|image=outdoorspace-monochrome.png}} {{Class Ψ|color-s=0%|safety=Varying Safety|entity=Irreversible Psychological Hazards}}
Level 336, otherwise known as "Undecided Fate" or "Senseless Salvation," is classified as the 337th level of the Backrooms. Under no circumstances should one intentionally try to set foot on this level, and wanderers are advised to report any instances of entry back to the nearest [[B.L.S.A.I.D]] department immediately. ---- ==General Description== A hallucinogenic recreation of a traveler's childhood memories is the defining feature of Level 336. While its appearance may vary depending on the person present, Level 336 mostly resembles a recollection of places one has been to, particularly areas they feel closely aligned with. If someone's memories of them overlap, the level itself will select some features from each one and combine those into its own image. While this may not seem hazardous at first glance, remembering many locations at the same time is suicidal, as Level 336's environment becomes increasingly unstable when it tries to collect and make use of such thoughts. Trying to disconnect oneself from recalling is highly encouraged, as that action will greatly increase their chances of survival until they manage to find an exit. Nursery themes linger across Level 336, though it is indeterminable where these sounds come from. As time progresses, the rhythm will become louder, more unsettling, and distorted, asserting a homesickness-like effect on an individual. Extended periods of exposure through the ears can result in gradual insanity, leaving one vulnerable to [[Phenomenon 35|the Wretched Cycle]]. Level 336 also possesses a monochromatic filter, coinciding with most levels affected by [[Come to Light (1/2)|Phenomenon 20]], leading to blurred vision upon entering and briefly after egress. In severe cases revolving around excessively long stays, this effect stays indefinitely, becoming a hindrance to a person's eyesight and causing visual impairments. Upon further traversal, artificial structures will begin to manifest based on the collected memories, appearing in the form of houses, playgrounds, or anything imaginable. These things provoke a nostalgic feeling inside a wanderer's mind, yet somehow they are eerie at the same time. Prolonged sighting should be avoided as it can cause significant disorientation, effectively preventing one from being aware of the dangers ahead. Entering these areas is also a death wish due to the risk of being stuck inside or sucked into an unknown level. Despite being under the cover of a seemingly safe level, Level 336 preys on the memories of those who enter its domain. It traps wanderers in an infinite loop of regret and suffering, initially letting one explore the level thoroughly before hitting them with a harsh reality afterwards. Gathering sufficient information about Level 336 has been proven to be taxing, or even, by some, infeasible. Even the usage of drones is futile, considering they immediately malfunction within the level's airspace. ==Mental Hazards== ===Billboards=== Billboards are signs that are randomly scattered across Level 336, spanning all directions, and can shift into any shape or form. They usually appear once the level's reassembly is completed, and how they affect wanderers is rather situational. If somebody doesn't have any long-lasting regrets or abysmally low self-esteem, billboards will display nothing at all, like a blank sheet of paper. This, however, is not an implication that these types of billboards are completely safe to view. Should one stare at them for too long, all of their memories will be wiped out, every single trace. Consequently, they may also be unable to leave Level 336 and risk succumbing to other hostilities. On the contrary, billboards pose an extreme threat to those affected by trauma and regret. Embedded on them is dehumanizing language used to collapse one's mental fortitude, and the messages become more cluttered the longer a person is stuck inside Level 336. Additionally, multiple speakers placed on these billboards can emit a loud, echoing noise, reciting the exact messages that appear on them. Some samples have been provided below: * "Are you insane? Look at yourself. Look at who you have become." * "It's pretty hilarious you survived the challenges up to now. Now meet your demise, subhuman." * "It's all your fault. Had you had more foresight, this charade wouldn't have happened." * "Do you see it now? Do you see what you did to yourself?" * "Escape is no salvation. You will be haunted for the rest of your life." To combat this, simply look away. Avoid any direct eye contact with the billboards. The urge to look at them will increasingly grow, so be prepared for a tough time. ===Spirits=== Spirits take on the appearance of one's former loved ones. They usually walk around Level 336 carrying something that is of dearest value to a lost wanderer. It is advisable that one should keep their distance from spirits, as the closer they are, the stronger the will to interact becomes. It is theorized that spirits exist to remind someone of their former self and happiness, or sometimes the complete opposite. Talking to spirits induces feelings of melancholy, desperation, and an intense desire to make peace with oneself again. Spirits tend to disappear fairly quick, which can prove helpful in certain cases. ===The Whispering Plague=== The Whispering Plague is a nerve agent formerly developed by M.E.G. scientist Dr. Josh Everstone in the confines of [[Level 181]]. Nevertheless, for unknown reasons, traces of this chemical weapon are visible throughout various corners of Level 336. These gases can come from all directions and be easily identified through the red smoke they emit. Inhaling the Whispering Plague causes hallucinations, lunatic-like behavior, and even organ failure. The [[M.E.G.]] is currently trying its best to retrieve and dispose any samples left behind in 181 as a result of this. ===What Could Have Been.===
'''Warning:'''
Data retrieval failed. '''Reason:'''
The following data is heavily corrupted and is currently undergoing restoral.
Estimated time of completion: [REDACTED]
==Entities== No entities were documented in Level 336. ==Colonies & Outposts== Most attempts at establishing one ended up in disaster, with members either going insane or completely losing their senses. It may be a wise idea to abandon ill-fated efforts to create something that won't last for even a week. ==Addendum==
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Having bad dreams frequently in [[Level 18]] might transport one to Level 336. *A drawing with the number 336 on it can seldom appear on the walls of [[Level 287]], interacting with it will lead to the former. *Travelling too deep into Section 3 of [[The Core]] has a chance of sending one to Level 336. *Parts of [[Level 818]] can shift into Level 336 when least expected. *Entering a church in [[Level 236]] and staying for 20 minutes can let one end up in Level 336. ===Exits=== There is only one confirmed exit: *Following the violin noise heard in the interior of Level 336's church will guide one to [[Level 483]].
Walking through an endless road.
Thinking again, peace is a no.
The day of hate, you don't forget.
Life is stuck, full of regrets.
The friends we made along the way.
Now we go on our own pathways.
It's just you that's left wondering.
Was this engineered to be coming?
The memories that somehow remained,
Only served to torment you again.
About how different things could have been.
About the person you could have become.
Easier to fall than to get up.
There is no way to move onwards.
Sometimes you just accept the truth.
That fate has left you alone.
It takes people too long to realize.
That life is a cycle of lies.
The death never happens to suffer.
That's why we are still here.
Join them, for that life will be done.
No more worries, no more regrets.
No longer have to be a burden.
A puppet in the eyes of the world.
